title: fix(core): move llm-based output processors off stream chunks
summary: Fixes a critical performance issue (#13644) where LLM-based output processors (PIIDetector, SystemPromptScrubber, ModerationProcessor) were making a full LLM API call for every streamed text-delta chunk, causing ~50x slowdown (186 tokens = 186 sequential LLM calls). The fix converts processOutputStream to a simple passthrough (return args.part) and adds processOutputStep methods so the LLM-based detection runs once per agentic step on the assembled output, not per token. Also cleans up UnicodeNormalizer's availablePhases to only declare processInput. Net deletion of ~277 lines of per-chunk LLM logic with updated tests to verify streaming is a no-op and step-level processing works correctly.
maintainerNotes: High-impact performance fix from an external contributor addressing a well-documented issue. The approach is sound: LLM-based processors should not run per-chunk. CodeRabbit flagged that the passthrough in processOutputStream could leak unredacted content to streaming clients before processOutputStep runs — this is a valid concern worth discussing, though the tradeoff is between security-during-stream and usability (150s vs 9s responses). PR description is empty (just the template) but the linked issue #13644 is extremely detailed with reproduction and benchmarks. CI checks are pending (stuck on secrets-gated jobs, which is normal for external forks). No merge conflicts. Has a changeset.
